From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Prototype Myanma bullpup assault rifle

| EMER-K1 | |
| --- | --- |
| Type | * [Bullpup](/wiki/Bullpup "Bullpup") [assault rifle](/wiki/Assault_rifle "Assault rifle") * Bullpup [light machine gun](/wiki/Light_machine_gun "Light machine gun") |
| Place of origin | [Myanmar](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar") |
| Service history | |
| In service | 1998-? |
| Used by | [Tatmadaw](/wiki/Tatmadaw "Tatmadaw") (primary user) |
| Production history | |
| Designer | Electrical Mechanical and Engineering Corps (EMEC) |
| Designed | 1995 |
| Manufacturer | Electrical Mechanical and Engineering Corps (EMEC) |
| Produced | 1995 |
| No. built | Preproduction prototypes only |
| Specifications | |
| Mass | * 4 kg (8.8 lb) (no magazine, assault rifle)[[1]](#cite_note-BMSA-1) * 4.5 kg (9.9 lb) (light machine gun)[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEJohnsonNelson2016218-2) |
| --- | |
| [Cartridge](/wiki/Cartridge_(firearms) "Cartridge (firearms)") | [5.56×45mm NATO](/wiki/5.56%C3%9745mm_NATO "5.56×45mm NATO") |
| [Action](/wiki/Action_(firearms) "Action (firearms)") | [Gas-operated](/wiki/Gas-operated_reloading "Gas-operated reloading"), [closed bolt](/wiki/Closed_bolt "Closed bolt") |
| [Rate of fire](/wiki/Rate_of_fire "Rate of fire") | 650 rounds/minute[[1]](#cite_note-BMSA-1) |
| Effective firing range | 400 m (440 yd)[[1]](#cite_note-BMSA-1) |
| Feed system | 30-round detachable [STANAG](/wiki/Standardization_Agreement "Standardization Agreement") [box magazine](/wiki/Magazine_(firearms)#Box "Magazine (firearms)") |
| Sights | Post front sight and adjustable rear sights |

The **EMER K-1**,[[3]](#cite_note-3) sometimes known as the **EMER K1**[[4]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEThompson201943-4) or **EMERK-1**,[[1]](#cite_note-BMSA-1) was a prototype [bullpup](/wiki/Bullpup "Bullpup") [assault rifle](/wiki/Assault_rifle "Assault rifle") made in [Myanmar](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar") by the Electrical Mechanical and Engineering Corps (EMEC).[[5]](#cite_note-5) [Reverse engineered](/wiki/Reverse_engineering "Reverse engineering") in 1995 from the Chinese [QBZ-97](/wiki/QBZ-97 "QBZ-97") assault rifle, the EMER K-1 was prone to stoppage.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=EMER-K1&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The EMER K-1's development started after Ng Chung-Keung, a subsidiary of Chinese defense contractor [Norinco](/wiki/Norinco "Norinco"), exported a batch of [QBZ-97](/wiki/QBZ-97 "QBZ-97") assault rifles to Myanmar.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-BTP-7) Norinco decided not to export any more QBZ-97s due to arms sanctions placed on the country.[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEJohnsonNelson2016218-2) Myanmar, in response, refused to return them and subsequently were used as the basis for [reverse engineering studies](/wiki/Reverse_engineering "Reverse engineering").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

It was designed by EMEC engineers in 1995 with a batch of 15-16 firearms made for testing and evaluation.[[9]](#cite_note-BSA-9)[[1]](#cite_note-BMSA-1)

Some EMER-K1s were issued to elite Myanmar troops on guard duty at the [Yadana gas field](/wiki/Yadana_gas_field "Yadana gas field"), according to [Yangon](/wiki/Yangon "Yangon")-based diplomats in 1998.[[1]](#cite_note-BMSA-1)[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEJohnsonNelson2016218-2)

The EMER-K1 was not adopted by the [Tatmadaw](/wiki/Tatmadaw "Tatmadaw") due to various problems on reliability and multiple incidents of stoppages and gun jams.[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEJohnsonNelson2016218-2) The QBZ-97 was reexamined in order to reverse engineer it once again.[[10]](#cite_note-MK_III_History-10) This time, the decision was made to use the QBZ-97 as the basis for the [MA-1 MK III](/wiki/MA-1_MK_III "MA-1 MK III") assault rifle family.[[10]](#cite_note-MK_III_History-10)

## Design

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=EMER-K1&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Design")]

Reports suggested that the development of the EMER-K1 was made possible due to technical assistance from [Singapore](/wiki/Singapore "Singapore"),[[9]](#cite_note-BSA-9) in violation of [United Nations](/wiki/United_Nations "United Nations") arms sanctions.[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEJohnsonNelson2016218-2) This was also made possible due to machinery allegedly supplied by Singapore.[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEJohnsonNelson2016218-2)

Construction of the EMER-K1s were done via [stamping](/wiki/Stamping_(metalworking) "Stamping (metalworking)") with all-metal bodies.[[1]](#cite_note-BMSA-1)[[11]](#cite_note-11) The design was based on other assault rifles like the [SAR21](/wiki/SAR21 "SAR21"), SA80, M16, [IMI Tavor](/wiki/IMI_Tavor "IMI Tavor") and the [Steyr AUG](/wiki/Steyr_AUG "Steyr AUG").[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEJohnsonNelson2016218-2)

The EMER-K1's [flash hider](/wiki/Flash_hider "Flash hider"), carry handle, magazine housing, bayonet stud, sling swivels and flip aperture sights are based on the [M16](/wiki/M16_rifle "M16 rifle") series.[[9]](#cite_note-BSA-9) The pistol grip, trigger, trigger guard, stock and vent holes are based on the [SA80](/wiki/SA80 "SA80") series.[[9]](#cite_note-BSA-9)

The rifle’s [action](/wiki/Action_(firearms) "Action (firearms)") appears to be based on the [QBZ-95](/wiki/QBZ-95 "QBZ-95")'s gas piston.[[9]](#cite_note-BSA-9) The fire selector has three settings and is positioned on the left side behind the magazine well with the charging handle position on the right side.[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEJohnsonNelson2016218-2)

## Variants

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=EMER-K1&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Variants")]

### EMER-K1 Assault Rifle

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=EMER-K1&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: EMER-K1 Assault Rifle")]

The basic version of the EMER-K1.[[9]](#cite_note-BSA-9) It, like the LMG version, are 832mm in length with effective range and firing rate as 400m and 650 rounds per minute.[[12]](#cite_note-BLD-12) Its weight is 4 kg.

### EMER-K1 LMG

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=EMER-K1&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: EMER-K1 LMG")]

An LMG version of the EMER-K1.[[9]](#cite_note-BSA-9) The barrel appears to be an AKM-based muzzle-climb compensator while having a different handguard.[[13]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEJohnsonNelson2016219-13) It is 500g heavier at 4.5kg with no magazine.[[12]](#cite_note-BLD-12)
